chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Prohibition of Hunting in the Sanctuary and Its Rulings

text: 2355 - وَ رَوَى اَلْعَلاَءُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يُحْرِمُ وَ عِنْدَهُ فِي أَهْلِهِ صَيْدٌ إِمَّا وَحْشٌ وَ إِمَّا طَيْرٌ قَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2355 - Al-'Ala narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who enters into ihram while he has wild animals or birds in his household.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it."
